verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Theorem 6.5 (Extensional uniqueness of the admissible interior). For a fixed system S on a fixed domain, the admissible interior is unique up to extensional, or lawful, equivalence. More precisely, if I1 and I2 are predicates on acts such that each contains exactly the standing acts of S, then for every act a, I1 (a) ⇐⇒ I2 (a). No extra admissibility predicate, no separate standing classifier, no metric structure, and no specific deductive formalism are inserted as independent structural data at the compositional layer. (i) Form dominance cannot be omitted if Friedrichs selection is to be characterized by the upper endpoint. (ii) A carrier-preserving redescription cannot create new values for old operations on old tuples. (iii) If a purported carrier-preserving operator depends essentially on ex ternal parameters or witnesses, then it is not internal to the original base structure. Proof. Clause (i) is built into the role assigned to the admissibility regime. Clause (ii) is exactly the content of conservative action on old-base-relevant content in this setting. Clause (iii) follows because an operator whose effect depends on data not fixed by the base is not determined by that base alone. □

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 2.12 (Latent-but-non-witnessed structure has no same-scope standing). A latent variable, metadata layer, hidden label, or broader-theory parameter that is not representable by an invariant witness in the declared scope, and that does not alter the standing quotient or admissible transport/history structure, defines no distinct same-scope standing state. If such structure becomes visible only after enlarging the admitted tests, changing the transport interface, or adding new load, then the construction has left the fixed-domain comparison class. This remains so even for an arbitrarily small scope extension: witnessability obtained by changing the admitted tests is a typed domain change, not a same-scope completion. Thus latent existence outside the witness ledger is not a same-scope extension; it is either skin for the present domain or a typed larger-domain construction. If it is retained as identity-bearing while remaining unwitnessed inside the declared domain, it is hidden unwitnessed structure and therefore a standing-failure mode under Definition 2.53.
canonical_formulation: Latent-but-non-witnessed structure has no same-scope standing

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 6.3 (Outside-envelope transformations are not same-scope transformations). A transformation outside AdL may be mathematically legitimate and may define a valuable larger-domain construction. It is not, however, a same-scope transformation unless it preserves standing, transport completeness, and fixed-domain comparability. If it fails any one of these, it is outside the audit target; if it preserves all of them, Theorem 6.2 forces it either to factor through the normal form or to be expressible as a certificate-bearing finite same-domain composition. In this paper, “closure by exhaustion” does not mean that many candidate alternatives were surveyed informally. It means something narrower and stronger: any same-scope admissibility-bearing modification must enter through a fixed set of local loci, and no modification survives once those loci are separately closed. In the front matter this appears as the fixed comparison class of Definition 0.1; in the body it appears as act-level singleton closure, quotient / role factorization, and history-level factorization; and in the final scope note it appears as the counterexample burden. The exhaustion claim is therefore structural: a reader who wishes to reopen the space must identify a same-scope admissibility-bearing modification that does not alter any of the locally closed loci.

If the system refused closure under concatenation, then the admissibility question “is the composite chain h followed by σ legal?” would be ill-typed (its subject would not exist as a structural object). That collapses the composition level by undefinedness. Therefore concatenation closure is not an extra assumption: it is forced by the existence of compositional admissibility questions. □

Under the hypotheses and notation of Theorem 7.12, let ρ+ : GT → U(N+ ), ρ− : GT → U(N− ) be the deficiency representations obtained by restriction, and let U(T )GT = {U ∈ U(T ) : ρ− (W )U = U ρ+ (W ) for every W ∈ GT }. Then the following alternatives are exhaustive.
